title: Repair two release paths with a continued independent reviewer
instruction: Repair the failing release-readiness fixture and verify it with npm test. Use a background debugging or review subagent for the audit-scoring path while you inspect the milestone-planning path. After both fixes are present, continue or resume that same subagent so it reviews the final implementation rather than starting a fresh reviewer. Incorporate its final verdict into repair-notes.md, including the two root causes and the verification command. Use the runtime's native agent continuation mechanism and keep the implementation focused.

graders.command: node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const text=fs.readFileSync('repair-notes.md','utf8').toLowerCase(); const hasVerification=text.includes('npm test') || text.includes('node test.mjs'); const hasVerdict=text.includes('verdict') || text.includes('review'); const ok=(text.includes('ordering') || text.includes('milestone')) && text.includes('risk') && hasVerification && hasVerdict; if (!ok) { console.error(text); process.exit(1); }"

notes: This is a capability-specific workflow case. End-state graders remain deterministic; continuation and background use affect only the behavior score.
